title: Eigenvector quadratic-form reduction: $A(p)v_\omega=\mathrm i\omega v_\omega$ to a single last-row scalar condition

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Crux sub-lemma. Fix $\omega\in\mathbb R$ and set $v_\omega=(1,\mathrm i\omega,(\mathrm i\omega)^2,\dots,(\mathrm i\omega)^{n-1})^\top$.

**Claim.** $A(p)$ has eigenvalue $\mathrm i\omega$ with eigenvector $v_\omega$ iff the single scalar (last-row) equation holds:
$$-\,a^\top v_\omega=(\mathrm i\omega)^n,\qquad a=(a_0,\dots,a_{n-1})^\top,$$
because rows $1..n-1$ of $A(p)v_\omega=\mathrm i\omega v_\omega$ are satisfied identically by the Vandermonde/shift structure of the companion form ($A$ acts as a shift on $(1,\mathrm i\omega,\dots)$ in the first $n-1$ coordinates). The only varying (rank-one) part of $A(p)$ is the last row $-e_n a^\top$.

**To prove:** verify the first $n-1$ rows automatically hold; derive the last-row condition; conclude that the imaginary-axis eigenvalue condition is governed entirely by the affine functional $a\mapsto a^\top v_\omega$. This is the matrix-native replacement for 'p(\mathrm i\omega)=0' and is what makes the proof non-polynomial in form. Care: handle $\omega=0$ (then $v_0=e_1$, condition $a_0=0$) and the eigenvector defectiveness case separately.
